sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Diabetic foot problems: prevention and management
sources.version: NICE guideline NG19, last updated 11 October 2019 with selected 2023 recommendation amendments shown on the page
sources.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
sources.population: Children, young people, and adults with diabetes, with age-specific foot-assessment recommendations.
sources.clinical_setting: Diabetes foot-risk assessment, active foot-problem documentation, multidisciplinary pathways, and follow-up.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for clinician examination of both feet, neuropathy, ischaemia, ulceration, callus, infection or inflammation, deformity, gangrene, Charcot context, risk classification already assigned, and foot-review documentation. It does not auto-classify risk or generate referral or treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.6 — frequency, bilateral foot examination, and clinician risk stratification
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting age-appropriate review, bilateral skin and sensory examination, perfusion, ulceration, callus, infection or inflammation, deformity, gangrene, Charcot features, and clinician-entered risk classification without calculating it.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.10–1.3.13 — specialist assessment, reassessment, and information
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting completed foot protection assessment, vascular or biomechanical review, clinician-selected reassessment, information already provided, and an entered follow-up plan.

archive.title: Diabetic foot problems: prevention and management
archive.publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
archive.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
archive.population: Children, young people, and adults with diabetes, with age-specific foot-assessment recommendations.
archive.setting: Diabetes foot-risk assessment, active foot-problem documentation, multidisciplinary pathways, and follow-up.

exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting age-appropriate review, bilateral skin and sensory examination, perfusion, ulceration, callus, infection or inflammation, deformity, gangrene, Charcot features, and clinician-entered risk classification without calculating it.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Suspected neurological conditions: recognition and referral
sources.population: Adults aged over 16 years with suspected neurological conditions.
sources.clinical_setting: Initial assessment and referral from primary or community care.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for selected adult dizziness, vertigo, limb weakness, sensory symptoms, and tremor referral features; not applicable to children and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.7 — dizziness and vertigo in adults
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports focal neurological and hearing red flags, acute vestibular syndrome, HINTS by trained clinicians, neuroimaging referral, and Hallpike assessment for positional vertigo.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.7.1–1.7.10 and 1.10.1–1.10.6 — limb weakness and sensory symptoms
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports sudden, progressive, fluctuating, unilateral, symmetrical, sensory-associated, and back-pain-associated weakness, urgent referral, and clinician-led imaging where specified.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.10.1–1.10.13 — sensory symptoms including tingling or numbness in adults
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports sudden unilateral numbness, rapidly progressive symmetrical sensory change with weakness or imbalance, recurrent sensory episodes, distal sensory distribution, reflex and gait context, selected metabolic tests, radicular symptoms, bowel or bladder context, and clinician-led referral.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.15.1–1.15.4 — tremor in adults
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports rest or asymmetric tremor, stiffness, slowness, balance or gait difficulty, symmetrical postural tremor, medicine, thyroid and alcohol review, functional impact, head tremor, and clinician-led referral.
